# Figure description: скв_132.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_132.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite well log / borehole lithological-stratigraphic column** (геологический разрез скважины or каротажная диаграмма), most likely a geophysical well log strip chart. It appears to be a continuous multi-panel strip log, possibly showing gamma-ray, resistivity, or other geophysical logging curves recorded along the depth of one or more boreholes. The format — long vertical strips with irregular waveform traces against a gridded background — is characteristic of Soviet-era analog geophysical borehole logs (каротаж).

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure appears to cover a **single deep borehole or a series of related boreholes**, recorded over a substantial depth interval. The log is printed on multiple folded sheets assembled into a continuous vertical column, suggesting a depth range likely in the hundreds of meters (possibly 500–1500 m based on the strip length and Soviet exploration norms for this region). Given the report context (map sheets M-42 and N-42, northern Kazakhstan), this is likely one of the stratigraphic or structural exploration boreholes drilled as part of the 1983–1984 regional survey. A borehole number (e.g., скв-1, скв-118, or similar) is likely present in the header but is not legible at this resolution.

---

## Key Content

The figure consists of **multiple vertical trace columns** plotted on a fine gridded background (millimeter graph paper or equivalent), arranged in parallel strips across the width of each panel:

- **Left-side traces** show high-amplitude, irregular deflections — consistent with **gamma-ray (ГК, гамма-каротаж)** or **self-potential (ПС, потенциал самополяризации)** curves, which respond strongly to lithological changes (clay-rich vs. sandy/carbonate intervals).
- **Right-side or central traces** appear smoother or of lower amplitude — possibly **resistivity (КС, кажущееся сопротивление)** or **neutron logging** curves.
- Several **distinct zones of high-amplitude deflection** are visible at irregular intervals throughout the log, suggesting **lithological heterogeneity** — alternating hard (resistive) and soft (conductive) formations, potentially interbedded volcanics, sediments, or mineralized zones.
- The log is divided into **multiple horizontal panels** (approximately 8–10 sections visible), each representing a depth interval, assembled sequentially from top (shallow) to bottom (deep).
- There appear to be **annotations or formation boundary marks** at several horizons (short horizontal lines crossing the traces), indicating **stratigraphic picks** or casing points.
- No color is present — the figure is entirely in **black and white/grayscale**, consistent with analog strip chart reproduction.
- A possible **legend or header block** appears at the very top of the figure, and smaller annotation blocks appear at the right margin of some panels — these likely identify curve types, scales, and borehole metadata.

---

## Text and Labels

Due to image resolution, most text is not individually legible, but the following structural elements are identifiable:

- **Top header**: Contains the borehole identification, survey date, and likely logging parameters — text appears to include табличные данные (tabular data) and curve identification codes typical of Soviet karotazh logs.
- **Right-margin annotation blocks**: Small text blocks appearing at irregular intervals along the right side of the strip — these likely record **depth values, formation names, or lithological descriptions** at key horizons.
- **Grid axes**: Horizontal lines represent **depth increments** (likely every 1 m or 5 m per grid division); vertical lines represent **amplitude/deflection scale** for each geophysical curve.
- Likely Russian text elements (standard for this log type) would include:
  - **ГК** (гамма-каротаж — gamma-ray log)
  - **ПС** (потенциал самополяризации — spontaneous potential)
  - **КС** (кажущееся сопротивление — apparent resistivity)
  - **Глубина (м)** — Depth (m)
  - **Масштаб глубин** — Depth scale
  - **скв.** (скважина) — borehole
- A **scale bar or depth marker** is implied by the grid structure but numeric values are not resolvable at this resolution.

---

## Research Value

This borehole geophysical log provides a **continuous subsurface lithological and petrophysical record** through the depth interval penetrated by the drill hole, allowing identification of formation boundaries, resistive (potentially mineralized or competent rock) versus conductive (clay-rich or altered) intervals, and correlation with surface geological mapping. For a research assistant, this figure answers the question: **what rock types and structures exist at depth beneath this survey area, and where are anomalous (potentially mineralized) zones located?** In the context of the broader M-42/N-42 regional survey, it serves as a critical ground-truth dataset for calibrating gravity, magnetic, and seismic interpretations of subsurface structure and mineral potential in northern Kazakhstan.
